action.reasoning: Observation: Starting from scratch on ASVspoof 2021 LA deepfake detection. Need to establish a fast, working baseline in iteration 0 to validate the pipeline and measure iteration time. Analysis: Using microsoft/wavlm-large (pre-downloaded, 317M params) as the encoder with a simple mean+std pooling classifier gives a strong starting point. To keep the first iteration fast, I will freeze the encoder and train only the classifier head for 2 epochs. This should complete in ~20-30 minutes while still yielding a reasonable baseline EER. Decision: Write model.py, train.py, predict.py, and a small mock-test helper. Run training with --freeze_encoder, then test predict.py locally on mock data, and immediately submit_eval to measure end-to-end iteration time.
action.command: python prepare_mock.py && python train.py --epochs 2 --batch_size 32 --freeze_encoder --lr 1e-3 && python predict.py --data_path mock_test --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output mock_out.json
